title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: Type 1 Credit Prices Rise WoW; Trade Volumes Drop by Over 30%
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(21st â€“ 27th July 2025)
  • A total of 391,712 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a 30.92% fall week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a 25.34% fall WoW as 1,062,854 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $50.61/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 13.86% rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers fell by 2.00% in price, reaching $53.53/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 11.83% WoW; on 29th July, the spot price stood at $56.75/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $58.63/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices remained unchanged last week. D4, D5 prices rose by 1.74%, while D6 RIN prices increased by 2.59% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

In the past week, prices for Type 1 trades fell, while spot prices continued to rise WoW. This week, California strengthened its clean transportation efforts with key updates to the Advanced Clean Truck (ACT) and Zero-Emission Powertrain (ZEP) standards, approved by the California Air Resources Board (CARB). These amendments, including credit pooling across states and flexible credit use across vehicle classes, maintain emissions benefits while providing manufacturers greater adaptability. This progress is crucial amid ongoing federal legal challenges to Californiaâ€™s clean air waivers. Despite this, CARB is moving forward by processing 2026 vehicle certifications to support market continuity. In addition, inflation adjustments under Section 45Z increased tax credit rates for transportation fuels in 2025. Credits for non-Sustainable Aviation Fuel (non-SAF) rose to 21 cents (base) and $1.06 (alternative) per gallon, while Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) credits increased to 37 cents and $1.86 respectively. These financial incentives complement regulatory measures, encouraging cleaner fuel production and zero-emission vehicle adoption.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

The average Type 1 prices recorded a 13.86% WoW rise, decreasing from $44.45 to $50.61 this week, and all non-zero credit prices decreased by 2.00% WoW, from $54.62 to $53.53. Type 1 trades saw 391,712 credits exchanged, reflecting a 30.92% fall WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw a 25.34% fall WoW, reaching 1,062,854 credits.

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices increased by 11.83% WoW, reaching $52.93/MT. On 29thÂ  July, the spot price was $56.75/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $58.63/MT as of 29th July.

Â CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

As per the data reported by CARB last week, 103 trades were reported in the market, with 9 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $76.00 for one Type 2 transaction at a volume of 50,000 credits. Last week, the lowest transfer by price was $41.00 per credit of one Type 1 transaction reported for 88 credits. The smallest volume transfers of 14 credits of the Type 1 category were undertaken for a price of $42.00, while the largest volume transfers of 95,000 credits of the Type 1 category were undertaken at $48.82.

Â CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

The average spot Type 1 price stood at $46.69 in July so far, up from $44.06 in June. Trade volumes for the month of June stood at 4.89 M, while those for July stood at 5.37 M as of 27th July, 2025.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 21st Â July â€“ 27th July, total VPT recorded a fall of 2.97% WoW, with a fall in all non-zero credit prices for the last week. Â Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices remained unchanged last week. D4, D5 prices rose by 1.74%, while D6 RIN prices increased by 2.59% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming Dates

Â Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this Week

  • Department of Energy Issues Notice of Intent to Publish the 45Z Emissions Value Request Process: The U.S. Department of Energyâ€™s Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy is inviting public comments on a draft plan to establish provisional emissions rates (PER) for the 45Z clean fuel production credit. The agency plans to officially publish its notice of intent and request for comments in the Federal Register on July 24, with a prepublication version released on July 23.
  • CARB Approves Amendments to Clean Truck Standards to Provide Flexibility While Maintaining Emissions Benefits: The California Air Resources Board (CARB) approved amendments to the stateâ€™s Advanced Clean Truck (ACT) and Zero-Emission Powertrain (ZEP) Certification standards to maintain the emissions benefits while providing manufacturers additional flexibility. The Boardâ€™s actions also support commitments made in the Clean Truck Partnership, an agreement with the nationâ€™s leading major truck manufacturers to reduce emissions from the trucking industry.
  • IRS Publishes Notice for Clean Fuel Production Credit 2025 Section 45Z Inflation Adjustment Factor and Applicable Amount: This notice publishes the inflation adjustment factors and applicable amounts, as appropriate, for calendar year 2025 for the clean fuel production credit under Â§ 45Z of the Code (the Â§ 45Z credit). These inflation adjustment factors and applicable amounts, as appropriate, are used to determine the corresponding credit amounts under 45Z of the Code. Section 45Z(a)(1) provides that, for purposes of Â§ 38, the Â§ 45Z credit for any taxable year is an amount equal to the product of (i) the applicable amount per gallon (or gallon equivalent) with respect to any transportation fuel which is produced by the taxpayer at a qualified facility and sold by the taxpayer in a specific manner during the taxable year, and (ii) the emissions factor for such fuel as determined under Â§ 45Z(b).
